reasoning: The option-adjusted spread (OAS) for the ICE BofA US Corporate Index has been flat at 0.79% since Dec 23, 2025. Although 0.79 is historically tight, recent momentum is flat-to-downward, and multiple 2025/2026 market outlooks (e.g., Invesco) suggest spreads have room to tighten further toward the 0.55-0.70% range. The FRED short-term linear model gives a very low probability of an increase by Jan 11 (P=0.29), though January issuance could cause minor temporary widening, raising the combined estimate to ~0.48. Given the flat-to-tightening trend, the probability of an increase (value strictly > 0.79) is estimated to be slightly below 50% in the short term. Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty should increase with horizon (probabilities for distant dates must be closer to 0.5), the estimates are set to smoothly converge toward 50% as the forecast extends to April.
